'''Oliver Wilkes''' (born 2 May 1980) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby League]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} and {{rlp|lf}} in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s.

He has played for the [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Leigh Centurions]] (two spells), [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref name="Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|title=Heritage Numbers - In Debut Order|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327102545/http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=1822|archive-date=27 March 2019|url-status=dead}}</ref> [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (two spells), [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], [[Barrow Raiders]] and [[Workington Town]].

==Background==
Wilkes was born in [[Ulverston]], [[Cumbria]], England.

==Playing career==
===Sheffield Eagles===
Wilkes began his career at [[Sheffield Eagles]], making his [[Super League]] début as an 18-year-old in 1998.{{cn|date=April 2023}}

===Huddersfield Giants===
He also played in Super League for [[Huddersfield Giants]].{{cn|date=April 2023}}

===Keighley Cougars===
Wilkes then joined the [[Keighley Cougars]] in the [[Northern Ford Premiership]].{{cn|date=April 2023}}

===Leigh Centurions===
He then spent time at [[Leigh Centurions]], featuring for them in Super League in 2005.{{cn|date=April 2023}}

===Wigan Warriors===
Wilkes briefly returned to Super League with [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2006. In 2022, Wilkes admitted that he had taken performance-enhancing drugs at his previous club, [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], prior to his to move to Wigan.<ref>{{cite web |title=Former international player Oliver Wilkes admits performance-enhancing drug use |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/ukad-rugby-league-rugby-football-league-wigan-huddersfield-b2016153.html |website=The Independent |access-date=5 July 2022 |date=16 February 2022}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
His next move was to the [[Widnes Vikings]]. He played for Widnes Vikings in the [[Rugby League National Leagues in 2006#Grand Final|2006 National League Grand Final]]. After the season, he required reconstructive shoulder surgery.<ref name="Oliver Wilkes">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=28|title=Oliver Wilkes|publisher=Widnes|date=2007-07-20|access-date=2007-07-20|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070927201226/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=28 <!-- Bot retrieved archive -->|archive-date = 2007-09-27}}</ref> In 2007, he scored 15 tries in 34 games with Widnes Vikings before returning to Super League with Wakefield Trinity Wildcats the following year.<ref name="Player Stats">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php|title=Player Stats|publisher=Widnes|date=2007-07-20|access-date=2007-07-20|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070621200401/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php <!-- Bot retrieved archive -->|archive-date = 2007-06-21}}</ref>
[[File:Oliver Wilkes.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Wilkes in action for the Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
He returned to the Super League with Wakefield Trinity Wildcatsin 2007.<ref name="Player Stats">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php|title=Player Stats|publisher=Widnes|date=2007-07-20|access-date=2007-07-20|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070621200401/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php <!-- Bot retrieved archive -->|archive-date = 2007-06-21}}</ref>

Wilkes was a consistent feature in the 2009 [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] squad, and they managed to secure a fifth-place finish within the Super League reaching the play-offs. 
[[File:Oliver Wilkes Quins.JPG|thumb|right|Wilkes in action for Harlequins Rugby League]]

===Harlequins Rugby League===
He then moved to [[London Broncos|Harlequins RL]], spending two years there before returning to Wakefield Trinity Wildcats.{{cn|date=April 2023}}

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
Wilkes rejoined Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in 2012.

===Leigh===
Wilkes left Wakefield Trinity Wildcats to rejoin Leigh in 2014, captained the side Leigh to the [[2014 RFL Championship|League Leaders' Shield]], and the Championship Grand Final victory. At the end of the 2015 season, he announced he was leaving Leigh to sign for his hometown club [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]].{{cn|date=April 2023}}

===Barrow Raiders===
Wilkes left the [[Barrow Raiders]] after spending the 2016 and 2017 seasons at the [[RFL League 1|League 1]] club.

===Workington Town===
Wilkes joined Workington Town ahead of the 2018 season.
He was named club captain for the 2018 season, he played for town for 2 years making 43 appearances and scoring 18 tries, Wilkes announced his retirement in August 2019.<ref>{{cite web |title=My 21 years in Rugby League - Oliver Wilkes |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/55605/my--years-in-rugby-league---oliver-wilkes |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=5 July 2022 |date=28 August 2019}}</ref>

==International career==
He was named to the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Scotland|Scotland]] training squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]]<ref name="Henderson trio in Scots cup squad">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7587971.stm|title=Henderson trio in Scots cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-08-29|access-date=2008-10-03}}</ref> and was named to the full Scotland squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]]. He scored the winning try over [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in Scotland's first ever World Cup win.<ref name="Logan back in Scotland cup squad">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7655662.stm|title=Logan back in Scotland cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref> Wilkes was recalled to the Scotland squad in 2018, at the age of 38, for the European championships.
